description_long: This is a traditional Japanese katana displayed on a wooden stand, with both the sword and its matching sheath visible. The katana features a gently curved, single-edged blade with a dark, polished finish and a visible hamon line. The handle is wrapped in a light-colored material, likely ray skin or cloth, and is capped with gold or brass fittings, including a decorative tsuba (guard). The sheath is similarly adorned, matching the handle in color and featuring gold or brass accents at the ends. The stand is ornately carved and rests on a circular wooden platform with a gold rim, enhancing the display's elegance. The overall presentation suggests ceremonial or decorative use, with attention to both function and artistry.
description: A traditional Japanese katana with a curved blade and ornate handle, displayed on a carved wooden stand with a matching sheath. The set features gold or brass accents and a polished, elegant finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the katana unsheathed on a wooden stand, with the blade and sheath both fully visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the katana and sheath, with the stand and circular base visible from the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the katana and sheath on the stand, highlighting the symmetry and decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thinness of the sword and sheath, with the stand's structure more apparent.
attribute_handle_design: straight, light-colored wrap with gold/brass fittings and decorative end cap
attribute_ornamentation: decorative fittings, patterned stand, gold/brass accents
